================================================================================
EXTENSION PANEL ANIMATION — REFERENCE (for reading / learning)
================================================================================
Source of truth in the repo:
  src/components/extensions/InstalledExtensionsPanel.tsx

Below is the animation-related logic only (constants, state machine, JSX that
drives motion). Line numbers match the file as of this document’s creation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1) TIMING CONSTANTS (lines 7–10)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/** List column contracts/expands on click (matches `duration-300` / 300ms CSS). */
const LIST_CONTRACT_MS = 300;
/** Detail panel fades in after list contraction finishes. */
const PANEL_FADE_MS = 280;


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2) STATE + TIMERS (lines 161–167, 169–174, 176–217, 219)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function InstalledExtensionsPanel() {
  const [layoutNarrowed, setLayoutNarrowed] = useState(false);
  /** Panel column width: stays open while opacity fades out on close. */
  const [panelTrackOpen, setPanelTrackOpen] = useState(false);
  const [panelRevealed, setPanelRevealed] = useState(false);
  const openTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);
  const closeTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    return () => {
      if (openTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(openTimerRef.current);
      if (closeTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(closeTimerRef.current);
    };
  }, []);

  const togglePython = useCallback(() => {
    if (panelRevealed) {
      if (openTimerRef.current) {
        clearTimeout(openTimerRef.current);
        openTimerRef.current = null;
      }
      setPanelRevealed(false);
      if (closeTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(closeTimerRef.current);
      closeTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => {
        setPanelTrackOpen(false);
        setLayoutNarrowed(false);
        closeTimerRef.current = null;
      }, PANEL_FADE_MS);
      return;
    }

    if (layoutNarrowed) {
      if (closeTimerRef.current) {
        clearTimeout(closeTimerRef.current);
        closeTimerRef.current = null;
        setPanelRevealed(true);
        return;
      }
      if (openTimerRef.current) {
        clearTimeout(openTimerRef.current);
        openTimerRef.current = null;
      }
      setLayoutNarrowed(false);
      return;
    }

    if (closeTimerRef.current) {
      clearTimeout(closeTimerRef.current);
      closeTimerRef.current = null;
    }
    setLayoutNarrowed(true);
    openTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => {
      setPanelTrackOpen(true);
      setPanelRevealed(true);
      openTimerRef.current = null;
    }, LIST_CONTRACT_MS);
  }, [layoutNarrowed, panelRevealed]);

  const rowOpen = layoutNarrowed || panelTrackOpen;


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3) OPEN / CLOSE SEQUENCE (plain English)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OPEN (click Python row when fully closed):
  A) setLayoutNarrowed(true)
     → List column style maxWidth becomes "60.5rem"
     → CSS transition on that div: transition-[max-width] duration-300 ease-out
  B) After LIST_CONTRACT_MS (300 ms):
     → setPanelTrackOpen(true)  → outer detail wrapper maxWidth "43.25rem" (instant; see inline transition)
     → setPanelRevealed(true)   → inner wrapper opacity 1 with PANEL_FADE_MS transition

CLOSE (click again when panel visible):
  A) setPanelRevealed(false) → opacity fades out over PANEL_FADE_MS
  B) After PANEL_FADE_MS → setPanelTrackOpen(false); setLayoutNarrowed(false)
     → Panel column maxWidth animates to 0 over LIST_CONTRACT_MS (ease-out)
     → List column maxWidth back to "103.25rem" with the same 300 ms Tailwind transition


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4) JSX — ROW CONTAINER (gap) (lines 226–232)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

      <div
        className={`box-border flex min-h-0 min-w-0 w-full shrink-0 flex-1 flex-row overflow-hidden px-[2rem] pb-[2rem] transition-[gap] duration-300 ease-out ${
          rowOpen ? "" : "max-w-[103.25rem]"
        }`}
        style={{
          gap: rowOpen ? "1rem" : "0",
        }}
      >


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5) JSX — LIST COLUMN (contraction / expansion) (lines 234–240)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

        <div
          className={`flex min-h-0 min-w-0 flex-col overflow-hidden transition-[max-width] duration-300 ease-out motion-reduce:transition-none ${
            layoutNarrowed ? "shrink-0" : "w-full flex-1"
          }`}
          style={{
            maxWidth: layoutNarrowed ? "60.5rem" : "103.25rem",
          }}
        >

The scrollable list lives inside this div (header + div.overflow-y-auto).


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6) JSX — DETAIL PANEL SLOT + FADE (lines 286–306)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

        <div
          className={`shrink-0 overflow-hidden ${
            panelRevealed ? "" : "pointer-events-none"
          }`}
          style={{
            maxWidth: panelTrackOpen ? "43.25rem" : "0",
            transition: `max-width ${panelTrackOpen ? 0 : LIST_CONTRACT_MS}ms ease-out`,
          }}
          aria-hidden={!panelRevealed}
          inert={panelRevealed ? undefined : true}
        >
          <div
            style={{
              width: "43.25rem",
              opacity: panelRevealed ? 1 : 0,
              transition: `opacity ${PANEL_FADE_MS}ms ease-out`,
            }}
          >
            <ExtensionDetailPanel />
          </div>
        </div>


================================================================================
MAKING THE CONTRACTION *FEEL* SLOWER WHILE STAYING @ 300 ms
================================================================================
Duration stays 300 ms; “feels slower” = more even motion / more visible change.

1) EASING — ease-out front-loads motion
   - ease-out: fast at the start, slow at the end → reads as “snapped then crept”.
   - Try ease-in-out or a symmetric cubic-bezier so velocity is lower at t=0
     Example: cubic-bezier(0.45, 0, 0.55, 1) or Tailwind ease-in-out.
   - “Slower start” specifically: ease-in (slow start, fast end) can feel odd
     for a close; often ease-in-out is the best tradeoff at the same 300 ms.

2) ANIMATE WHAT ACTUALLY MOVES IN PX
   - You animate max-width; visible width only changes when max-width crosses
     below the width the flex row would otherwise assign. If the parent is
     narrower than both caps, almost nothing moves.
   - Path: drive an explicit width or flex-basis from measured pixels, or use
     transform: scaleX on a wrapper (different tradeoffs), so the eye always
     sees motion proportional to the easing curve.

3) BIGGER PIXEL DELTA AT THE SAME 300 ms
   - Same duration with a larger start→end distance increases average speed
     in px/s but can paradoxically read as “more deliberate” if motion was
     previously sub-pixel. Ensure the narrow target actually binds in your
     viewport (or use % widths relative to #coding).

4) OPTIONAL — SLIGHTLY LONGER DURATION
   - True “slower” motion = more ms. If you must keep exactly 300 ms, easing +
     animating the right property are the levers.

5) OPTIONAL — STAGGER
   - Tiny delay before max-width starts (e.g. requestAnimationFrame × 2) can
     separate click feedback from motion; does not lengthen the 300 ms ramp
     unless you add dead time.

6) REDUCED MOTION
   - motion-reduce:transition-none is already on the list column; respect that
     for any new curves or durations.

================================================================================
END
================================================================================
